input.message: feat(themes): wire dark mode into theme loader
test(themes): add dark mode test stub

input.title: Add dark mode theme
input.body:
## Summary

This PR adds dark mode theme support to the application.

### Changes

1. **feat(themes): add dark mode stylesheet** - Introduces `src/themes/dark.css` with dark mode color variables (background, foreground, and accent colors)
2. **feat(themes): wire dark mode into theme loader** - Updates `src/themes/index.css` to import the new dark mode stylesheet
3. **test(themes): add dark mode test stub** - Adds initial test file `tests/test_dark_mode.py` with placeholder for theme loader integration

The dark mode uses CSS custom properties scoped to `[data-theme='dark']` for easy theme switching.
This breaks when `token` is None — add an early return.
Use TOKEN_TTL_SECONDS constant from config.py instead of hard-coding 3600.
Add a test for the expired-token branch.
Three issues — see inline. Otherwise the structure is good.
Heads up — the auth module also needs unit tests for the new code path. Happy to pair on it if useful.

# Changelog

## 0.4.0 (2026-05-15)

- Add retry-with-backoff to the OpenAI client
- Fix incorrect cost-per-token for Haiku 4.5
- Drop Python 3.11 support

## 0.3.0 (2026-04-22)

- Initial public release of the scratch repo
- Add caching layer for the Anthropic client

input.title: fix: handle missing config gracefully
input.body: This fix adds a guard clause in the `load_config` function to raise a clean `FileNotFoundError` when the YAML configuration file is missing or empty. Previously, the function would fail with an unhelpful error when `read_yaml` returned `None`. Now it explicitly checks for this condition and raises a descriptive error message that includes the path to the missing config file, making it easier for users to diagnose and fix configuration issues.
input.head: fix/null-config-handling
